title: The One With The Blind Dates

jokes.text: You remembered to put clothes on this morning.
jokes.setup: Morning greeting between roommates
jokes.punchline: Implies Rachel has been forgetting to dress herself
jokes.explanation: Good economy and natural character moment, sets up Rachel's disoriented new mom state

jokes.text: Fifth day's a charm.
jokes.setup: Response to praise about remembering clothes
jokes.punchline: Twist on 'third time's a charm' reveals it took five attempts
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and escalation - reveals the absurd extent of her forgetfulness with perfect wordplay

jokes.text: And when does she stop crying all night?
jokes.setup: Being supportive about Rachel staying
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals the downsides
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica - supportive facade immediately cracking to reveal selfish concerns

jokes.text: Hey! You're not naked.
jokes.setup: Continuation of the naked/clothes theme
jokes.punchline: Joey's surprise at her being clothed
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that feels natural for Joey's character

jokes.text: She's probably exhausted from all that adorable screaming she did last night.
jokes.setup: Explaining why baby is sleeping
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic 'adorable' modifier on annoying behavior
jokes.explanation: Good use of ironic modifier to express frustration while maintaining politeness

jokes.text: when you walk in on a woman using a breast pump.. Yeah, that'll do it.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining how his romantic feelings are over
jokes.punchline: Specific awkward scenario that killed romance
jokes.explanation: Funny because it's specific, relatable, and effectively kills romantic mood

jokes.text: Well, actually it.. It's not a divorce. It is not a divorce!
jokes.setup: Ross saying breakup isn't like divorce
jokes.punchline: Chandler starts to correct him, Ross panics and overcompensates
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross panic response - his defensive overreaction when touched on sensitive topic

jokes.text: Names?
jokes.setup: Ross asking for specific names of women to date
jokes.punchline: Chandler's panicked realization he has no actual suggestions
jokes.explanation: Single word perfectly captures Chandler realizing he's in over his head

jokes.text: We haven't tried that yet! Goody!
jokes.setup: Monica offering strained plums baby food
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic enthusiasm for terrible food
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm in the face of domestic absurdity

jokes.text: What is there, like, two bites in here?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Ross and Rachel's situation
jokes.punchline: Chandler distracted by baby food portion size
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation about overpriced baby products delivered with Chandler timing

jokes.text: I may not have kids. Someone's gotta carry on the family name.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Ross and Rachel name a kid Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey's logic for why they should honor him
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey logic - self-centered but endearingly innocent reasoning

jokes.text: I had this date last night. Yuck! But we should probably keep it down, she's still in the bedroom.
jokes.setup: Joey complaining about bad date
jokes.punchline: Reveals the bad date is still there
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - completely reframes our understanding of the situation

jokes.text: You know what the best part of it is? I get to do my plan laugh.
jokes.setup: Joey excited about their scheme
jokes.punchline: Most excited about doing evil laugh
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about villain tropes while staying in Joey's childlike character

jokes.text: [Joey does evil laugh, woman emerges looking disheveled]
jokes.setup: Joey's dramatic evil laugh
jokes.punchline: His one-night stand emerges at worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - visual payoff to earlier setup with maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: I will have you know that we are very hip, happening people. Now if you'll excuse me, I have to get back to reading the obituaries.
jokes.setup: Monica defending their coolness
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself with elderly activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure - builds up defense then immediately undercuts it with absurd contradiction

jokes.text: You want a job? Turn off Oprah and send out a resume.
jokes.setup: Monica giving relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Shifts to practical career advice
jokes.explanation: Monica's controlling nature expressed through unsolicited practical advice

jokes.text: Now that she eats solid food, she poops around the clock.
jokes.setup: Rachel giving babysitting instructions
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about baby's bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Typical new parent oversharing that makes childless people uncomfortable

jokes.text: Suddenly I wish I was reading my own name.
jokes.setup: After hearing about baby care responsibilities
jokes.punchline: References obituaries Monica was reading
jokes.explanation: Dark humor callback with perfect economy - death preferable to babysitting duties

jokes.text: By 'massage' I mean hold down so he wouldn't turn over and flash me.
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing her massage client
jokes.punchline: Reveals disturbing reality of the 'massage'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reframes professional relationship as restraining dangerous person

jokes.text: Come on, who loves puzzles? Well, Ross does.
jokes.setup: Joey describing boring woman
jokes.punchline: Realizes she's perfect for Ross
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Joey's dim realization he's sabotaging their own plan

jokes.text: I'm not allowed to know smart women? Joey? We met at the library. I went in to pee.
jokes.setup: Phoebe questioning how Joey knows intellectual woman
jokes.punchline: Joey's reason for being at library
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - accidental meeting due to basic bodily needs, not intellectual pursuits

jokes.text: It's not Santa's plan. No, it's..
jokes.setup: Joey doing evil laugh for 'the plan'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's confusion about what plan he's referencing
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's literal thinking clashes with Joey's dramatic villain moment

jokes.text: Yeah, you know, it's not that fun. No, I think we killed it.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to do evil laugh
jokes.punchline: Admitting the bit isn't working
jokes.explanation: Meta-acknowledgment that their comedic bit has run its course

jokes.text: Don't cry, it's just a bit! I'm your Uncle Chandler. Funny is all I have!
jokes.setup: Chandler made baby cry with peekaboo
jokes.punchline: Desperate plea revealing his insecurity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler vulnerability - comedy as defense mechanism laid bare in crisis moment

jokes.text: I don't do that. I'll try to stop.
jokes.setup: Monica telling him not to masturbate
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately contradicting his denial
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom structure - immediate contradiction reveals the truth

jokes.text: I may have also served some very questionable meat at the restaurant.
jokes.setup: Monica panicking about missing ovulation window
jokes.punchline: Reveals another time-related disaster
jokes.explanation: Monica's perfectionism cracking under pressure - multiple disasters when timing fails

jokes.text: I'm not working! There's not much to do around here!
jokes.setup: Explaining why he used all the ovulation tests
jokes.punchline: Pathetic justification for inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Chandler's boredom leading to inappropriate curiosity - relatable but absurd

jokes.text: Well, if I'm still here in an hour, buy him a drink on me.
jokes.setup: Learning only a drunk Chinese guy is at the bar
jokes.punchline: Ross's resigned acceptance of his dating prospects
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about lowered expectations - Ross accepting he might get that desperate

jokes.text: I don't want to be drunk when I.. go home alone tonight.
jokes.setup: Rachel considering another drink
jokes.punchline: Pessimistic assumption about date outcome
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor about romantic prospects with good misdirection

jokes.text: Are you worried your date came, saw you and left?
jokes.setup: Ross explaining he got stood up
jokes.punchline: Waitress suggesting even worse scenario
jokes.explanation: Brutal honesty from service worker - making bad situation worse

jokes.text: Oh, good, because as of 4:00 this afternoon, I am not.
jokes.setup: Monica saying she's still ovulating
jokes.punchline: Chandler's precise timing about his sexual readiness
jokes.explanation: TMI humor with specific timing - Chandler's awkward oversharing about sexual function

jokes.text: I know you're not 18 anymore, but give it a minute.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he can't perform
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical solution ignoring his feelings
jokes.explanation: Monica's goal-oriented approach to intimacy - treating sex like a problem to solve

jokes.text: Canoodling?
jokes.setup: Monica using euphemism for sex
jokes.punchline: Chandler's disbelief at the word choice
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction to archaic euphemism - Chandler's modern sensibility vs Monica's propriety

jokes.text: Well, I can't say hump or screw.. in front of the B-A-B-Y.
jokes.setup: Explaining euphemism choice
jokes.punchline: Says crude words while spelling 'baby'
jokes.explanation: Ironic moment - says vulgar words to avoid saying them, while baby-talking

jokes.text: Well, what kind of a sick bastard wants to do it in front of a deer?
jokes.setup: Monica referencing bed-and-breakfast deer incident
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defensive overreaction
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Chandler's indignation at being judged for his boundaries

jokes.text: I'm kind of funny-looking.
jokes.setup: Rachel complimenting his looks
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating response
jokes.explanation: Basic self-deprecation that establishes character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Well, I'm not.
jokes.setup: Suggesting he must be rich to be with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Deflating that possibility too
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of self-deprecation - systematic elimination of any positive qualities

jokes.text: Well, come on, Steve, let's not rule out nervous laughter.
jokes.setup: Steve saying he's not funny
jokes.punchline: Rachel finding silver lining in uncomfortable situation
jokes.explanation: Clever line that acknowledges the awkwardness while trying to salvage the situation

jokes.text: I have a lot of medical problems.
jokes.setup: Continuing list of negative qualities
jokes.punchline: Ominous health revelation
jokes.explanation: Continues escalating pattern of self-sabotage with vague but concerning detail

jokes.text: I lost it to drugs.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to find positive about restaurant ownership
jokes.punchline: Reveals he lost restaurant to addiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - completely reframes entrepreneurial success as addiction consequence

jokes.text: It's really fulfilling doing something you hate for no money.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking about T-shirt work
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic description of unfulfilling job
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic summation of working-class struggle with dark humor

jokes.text: And I'm pretty sure I'm infertile.
jokes.setup: Listing reasons he's a bad catch
jokes.punchline: Most personal and devastating revelation
jokes.explanation: Peak of self-sabotage escalation - moves from external to deeply personal problems

jokes.text: I do like my hair.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking what he likes about himself
jokes.punchline: Only positive thing is appearance-based (presumably bad hair)
jokes.explanation: Visual gag payoff - audience sees his presumably terrible hair that he thinks is good

jokes.text: Really?
jokes.setup: Steve claiming to like his hair
jokes.punchline: Rachel's incredulous response to what she sees
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - single word conveys visual information to audience

jokes.text: I'm going to hunt you down and kill you.
jokes.setup: Rachel calling Phoebe about bad date
jokes.punchline: Extreme threat over dating setup
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom escalation - hyperbolic threat over minor inconvenience

jokes.text: You know, you are talking about one of my dear, dear friends.
jokes.setup: Rachel complaining about Steve
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's fake defense of someone she set up maliciously
jokes.explanation: Good ironic defense when Phoebe knows exactly how bad Steve is

jokes.text: All right, so he gets a little crazy when he's stoned. He's not stoned. Did he go out for a cigarette?
jokes.setup: Phoebe making excuses for Steve's behavior
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's using drugs right now
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - reframes multiple bathroom breaks as drug use

jokes.text: Oh, wait, that's the end of a different plan.
jokes.setup: Talking about escaping to Mexico after their scheme
jokes.punchline: Realizes she's mixing up criminal plans
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe moment - casually reveals she has multiple criminal schemes

jokes.text: Emma was doing it.
jokes.setup: Monica asking why he's playing peekaboo alone
jokes.punchline: Blaming infant for his behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deflection - shifting responsibility to someone who can't argue back

jokes.text: You can't have S-E-X when you're taking care of a B-A-B-I-E.
jokes.setup: Joey lecturing about inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Spells out words like talking to children
jokes.explanation: Joey adopting parental behavior while being hypocritical - ironic given his lifestyle

jokes.text: Although I was hoping to have sex tonight.
jokes.setup: Accepting free crab cakes after being stood up
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about sexual expectations to waitress
jokes.explanation: Ross's social awkwardness - inappropriate oversharing in professional interaction

jokes.text: We have a bet on how long it'll take before you give up and go home.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about the waiters' pool
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're betting on his misery
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about service industry workers betting on customer humiliation

jokes.text: Well, that was weird. You were loud and I was fast.
jokes.setup: Talking about their rushed sexual encounter
jokes.punchline: Both admitting it was unsatisfying for different reasons
jokes.explanation: Good post-coital humor - both parties acknowledging mutual failure with TMI

jokes.text: You better hope we're pregnant.. because one way or another, we're giving a baby back to Rachel!
jokes.setup: Realizing Emma is missing
jokes.punchline: Threatening to steal a baby if they lost one
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Monica's desperation leading to criminal suggestions with perfect logic

jokes.text: Don't touch my coat.
jokes.setup: Rachel being understanding about him crying
jokes.punchline: Steve immediately rejecting her kindness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Steve sabotaging even moments of potential connection

jokes.text: We were playing peekaboo. She loves it when I'm dramatic.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he took Emma
jokes.punchline: Reframes kidnapping as performance art
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - turning inappropriate behavior into artistic expression

jokes.text: You are so lazy. Can't you get on top for once?
jokes.setup: Deducing they had sex from Chandler's hair
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate commentary on their sexual positions
jokes.explanation: Shocking Joey observation - too much information delivered with casual tone

jokes.text: Guys! Seriously, those sticks are expensive!
jokes.setup: Chandler mentioning ovulation test usage
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical concern about test costs
jokes.explanation: Monica's priorities - more upset about wasted money than inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: Unless you name your first-born child Joey.
jokes.setup: Joey threatening to tell Rachel
jokes.punchline: Blackmail demand for naming rights
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with escalation - Joey turning earlier conversation into blackmail

jokes.text: Your family name is Tribbiani.
jokes.setup: Joey wanting to carry on family name
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the name wouldn't continue anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect logic bomb - destroys Joey's entire argument with simple observation

jokes.text: You almost had me.
jokes.setup: Realizing his blackmail scheme is flawed
jokes.punchline: Admitting defeat with dignity
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment - gracefully accepting intellectual defeat

jokes.text: would you like to make love to me? Really, really not.
jokes.setup: Steve's pathetic proposition
jokes.punchline: Rachel's emphatic rejection
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with Rachel's brutal honesty delivered kindly

jokes.text: It doesn't work anyway.
jokes.setup: Being rejected for sex
jokes.punchline: Admitting impotence as final self-sabotage
jokes.explanation: Ultimate escalation - final devastating personal revelation with perfect economy

jokes.text: What size is that?
jokes.setup: Rachel showing inappropriate T-shirt gift
jokes.punchline: Ross asking about shirt size instead of being horrified
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - missing social cue and focusing on practical detail

jokes.text: Oh, so they owe me, like, three Phoebes.
jokes.setup: Learning about naming children after walking in on sex
jokes.punchline: Calculating how many times she's walked in on them
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe logic - treating babies like transaction rewards with perfect math

jokes.text: We're geniuses.
jokes.setup: Their plan backfiring completely
jokes.punchline: Joey still thinking they're smart
jokes.explanation: Good Joey obliviousness - complete inability to recognize failure

jokes.text: Mexico!
jokes.setup: Joey asking where to run
jokes.punchline: Phoebe defaulting to criminal escape plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Phoebe's criminal plans bleeding into current situation

jokes.text: Being stood up two nights in a row has left a crab cake sized hole in my heart.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to get more free food
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of heartbreak in food terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor - mixing romantic devastation with specific food imagery for comedic effect

jokes.text: I have such fat hands!
jokes.setup: Waiters betting on how long until someone cries
jokes.punchline: Man having breakdown about body image
jokes.explanation: Background gag that pays off betting setup with absurd self-criticism

jokes.text: I hate my dick!
jokes.setup: Continuing breakdown
jokes.punchline: Escalating to most intimate self-hatred
jokes.explanation: Crude escalation that goes for shock value rather than clever construction
